stemHtml: What are the characteristics of rime ice, and what conditions are most favorable for its formation?

options.a: Milky granular appearance, forming on leading edges and accumulating forward into the air stream. Stratiform clouds at temperatures of -10°C to -20°C are most conducive to its formation.
options.b: Smooth appearance and builds forward from leading surfaces into a sharp edge. Most common in cumuliform clouds at temperatures of -20°C to -25°C.
options.c: Opaque rough appearance, tending to spread back over an aircraft surface. Most frequently encountered in cumuliform clouds at temperatures slightly below freezing.
options.d: Transparent appearance and tendency to take the shape of the surface on which it freezes. Stratiform clouds and temperatures only slightly below freezing promote its formation.
